statement.markdown:
Given the root of a binary tree and an integer $k$, find the $k^{th}$ node from left to right.

You may print the node value.

This problem is a variation of the inorder traversal problem.
statement.inputFormat: The first line of input will contain two integers $n$ and $k$. The next $n$ lines will contain two integers $v$ and $x$, where $v$ represents the value of a node and $x$ represents the value of its parent.
statement.outputFormat: Print the value of the $k^{th}$ node from left to right.
statement.notes: You may assume that all nodes are correctly set in the binary tree. You may assume the $k^{th}$ node exists.

hints.text: Use a binary tree with DFS traversal.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: Find a way to traverse the binary tree, then print the kth node

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def __init__(self):
        self.root = None

    def solve(self):
        if not self.root: return
        stack = [self.root]
        idx = 0
        k = k-1
        while idx < k:
            node = stack[idx]
            idx += 1
            stack.append(node.left)
            stack.append(node.right)
        return stack[k].value

root = TreeNode(1)
root.left = TreeNode(2)
root.right = TreeNode(3)
root.left.left = TreeNode(4)
root.left.right = TreeNode(5)

solution = Solution()
print(solution.solve())
